body:
## Summary

Adds the **`paper-to-site`** skill — a workflow for turning an arXiv/LaTeX/review-style paper into a hosted `literature-template` site (paper as `/`, section tree, citations, figures, graph/backlinks), plus a standalone-repository workflow.

The skill was then **validated by blind-reproducing its canonical example** (arXiv 2605.06901, "Reflections and New Directions for Human-Centered LLMs"): running the skill from the arXiv source without consulting the existing hosted repo, then diffing. The blind run reproduced the existing site's file tree and `_meta.json` ordering exactly and ~99% of prose, and surfaced gaps that are now folded back into the skill.

## What the refinement pass added

- **Root page/layout** — documents the *three* layers that gate page chrome, not just one: component **conditions** (`library-page` / `not-index`), layout **`byPageType` positions** (`folder.positions.right: []` silently drops the section-index right sidebar), and index-specific **CSS** (`body[data-slug="index"]` special-casing). Includes the concrete `type: paper`-gated relaxation.
- **Figures and captions** — `<figure>/<figcaption>` or image + bold caption; don't wrap captions in emphasis (nested `\emph`); rewrite caption cross-refs.
- **Section index pages** — intro + figure + a `## Subsections` list.
- **Frontmatter** — list the full author set in the root paper note.
- **Graph tags** — conditional `showTags` guidance (avoid a single-paper `#paper` hub).

## Notes

- Also includes a small CI commit on this branch (`ci: honor AGENT_ENABLED in site preview workflow`).
- Documentation-only; no runtime/build changes. `npm run check` passes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body:
## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #18.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted human PR conversation feedback to learn from. PR #18 had only an automated `github-actions[bot]` preview comment, no reviews, and no inline comments. The PR body and diff describe project-specific skill guidance, but not a reusable user/team rubric beyond existing active rubrics like docs sync and audience-first documentation.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

title: Add paper-to-site skill (validated against arXiv 2605.06901)
